mission.title: longhorn: [BUG]  `instance-manager` on nodes that don't have hard or solid state disk DDOSing cluster DNS server with TXT query  `_grpc_config.localhost`
mission.description: [BUG]  `instance-manager` on nodes that don't have hard or solid state disk DDOSing cluster DNS server with TXT query  `_grpc_config.localhost`. Community-reported issue.

steps.title: Identify longhorn troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your longhorn deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n longhorn -l app.kubernetes.io/name=longhorn
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=longhorn -n longhorn --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review longhorn configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant longhorn configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n longhorn -l app.kubernetes.io/name=longhorn
kubectl get configmap -n longhorn -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=longhorn
```
### Describe the Bug

The instance managers on nodes that don't have hard drive keep flooding my DNS server with TXT DNS query `_grpc_config.localhost`.

steps.title: Apply the fix for [BUG]  `instance-manager` on nodes that don't have hard or…
steps.description:
since instance manager (plus the engine instance copied from engine image) are running inside a pod managed by the InstanceManager controller, it is not likely to disable such DNS queries using envvar `GRPC_ENABLE_TXT_SERVICE_CONFIG=false`. But you can still try the latest nightly build.

- Helm
```yaml
sudo kubectl apply -f https://github.com/longhorn/longhorn/releases/download/v1.10.1/longhorn.yaml
```

steps.title: Confirm [BUG]  `instance-manager` on nodes that don't… is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=longhorn -n longhorn --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n longhorn --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary: since instance manager (plus the engine instance copied from engine image) are running inside a pod managed by the InstanceManager controller, it is not likely to disable such DNS queries using envvar `GRPC_ENABLE_TXT_SERVICE_CONFIG=false`. But you can still try the latest nightly build.
codeSnippets:
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: tcpdump-pod
spec:
  hostNetwork: true  # Allows the pod to use the host network
  containers:
  - name: tcpdump
    image: nicolaka/netshoot:v0.15
    command:
      - "/bin/sh"
      - "-c"
      - "tcpdump -i cni0 -nn -v | grep '_grpc_config.localhost'"
    securityContext:
      privileged: true  # Required to run tcpdump with necessary permissions

tcpdump: listening on cni0, link-type EN10MB (Ethernet), snapshot length 262144 bytes

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with longhorn installed or the issue environment reproducible.
